parsed.production_notes: The video features a real human actor with natural facial expressions, realistic skin textures, and fluid body movements. The lighting, shadows, and background elements are consistent with a standard smartphone or webcam recording, showing no signs of AI generation.

characters.appearance: Young adult male of Asian descent with a medium build and tan complexion. He has short, spiky black hair and dark eyes.
characters.wardrobe: A beige and brown plaid button-up shirt worn unbuttoned over a plain white crew-neck t-shirt.

scenes.setting: A brightly lit room with a light grey wall and a white paneled door in the background.

panels.panel_visual_description: Character A stands centered in the frame, directly facing the camera. He uses active hand gestures to emphasize his points, occasionally raising his hands into view. His expression is earnest and enthusiastic. The background consists of a plain light grey wall on the left and a closed white paneled door on the right. A dark, rectangular object is partially visible mounted high on the wall to the left. The lighting is even and bright, casting soft, natural shadows on his face and the wall behind him.

A static text overlay is positioned at the top of the screen, remaining visible throughout. Large, dynamic text captions appear word-by-word in the center of the screen, synchronized with his speech. The background is a simple room setting with a grey wall and a white door. The overall composition focuses heavily on Character A's face and expressions. The bright lighting and clear audio create a vlog-style, conversational atmosphere.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Character A says: I gave my accent five minutes a day.
Character A says: Here's what happened after two weeks.
Character A says: I used to think fixing my pronunciation would take years of classes.
Character A says: Then I found out that the real problem was just a few words.
Character A says: All it took was five minutes a day, and that's less than one TikTok scroll session.

Character A says: I'm not trying to sound American, I just want to be understood the first time.
